session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8710 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the pr-feedback-classifier skill and use it with PR 8710.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll fetch the PR feedback for PR 8710 using the erk command:
session.tool_result:
{ "success": true, "pr_number": 8710, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8710", "pr_title": "Consolidate documentation from 15 erk-learn plans", "review_threads": [ { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5yE2q3", "path": "docs/learned/cli/activation-scripts.md", "line": 106, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — documents specific line numbers that will change.

Source: `src/erk/cli/activation.py`

Risk: Line number references (196-201, 202-225) go stale on any edit to `activation.py`. Consider replacing with a name-based source pointer instead:

```markdown
<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/activation.py, render_activation_script -->

`render_activation_script()` in `src/erk/cli/activation.py` generates the guard. `uv sync` and `uv pip install` run unconditionally before the guard, while venv activation, `.env` loading, and shell completion are inside the `VIRTUAL_ENV` guard.
```", "created_at": "2026-03-04T14:36:34Z" } ] }, { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5yE20u", "path": "docs/learned/erk/slot-pool-architecture.md", "line": 61, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — documents specific line numbers that will change.

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/slot/common.py`

Risk: The reference to `lines 179-181` will go stale on any edit above that point. Consider a name-based pointer:

```markdown
<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/slot/common.py, find_inactive_slot -->

`find_inactive_slot()` uses `git.status.get_file_status()` to distinguish staged/modified from untracked — only staged or modified files prevent reuse.
```", "created_at": "2026-03-04T14:36:43Z" }
... [50 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant:
Perfect. Now I'll classify the PR feedback according to the skill instructions. Let me analyze the data:
## Analysis
Based on the fetched PR feedback for PR 8710 ("Consolidate documentation from 15 erk-learn plans"), I can see:
**Review Threads (4 total):** 1. **Thread 1** (`docs/learned/cli/activation-scripts.md`, line 106): Bot comment about drift risk with line numbers (196-201, 202-225). Author: `github-actions[bot]` (bot) 2. **Thread 2** (`docs/learned/erk/slot-pool-architecture.md`, line 61): Bot comment about drift risk with line numbers (179-181). Author: `github-actions[bot]` (bot) 3. **Thread 3** (`docs/learned/erk/worktree-branch-mismatch.md`, line 28): Bot comment about verbatim code copy. Author: `github-actions[bot]` (bot) 4. **Thread 4** (`docs/learned/planning/planned-pr-lifecycle.md`, line 163): Bot comment about drift risk with file locations. Author: `github-actions[bot]` (bot)
**Discussion Comments (3 total):** - 2 metadata comments (submission-queued, workflow-started) - informational - 1 Audit PR Docs summary comment - informational
**File Restructuring:** No renames or moves detected (diff shows only modifications to existing files, no `-M` or `-C` indicators in file paths).
**Classification:** - All 4 review threads are from `github-actions[bot]` (bot authors) - All are code quality/documentation suggestions (drift risk, verbatim copies) - No file restructuring/moves detected in git diff - Therefore, `pre_existing: false` (not pre-existing because no restructuring AND these are specific to the documentation consolidation) - All threads are **actionable** - they're audit suggestions that require fixes - Complexity: All are **single_file** (each comment targets one file with specific line/section)
Here is the complete JSON output:

"thread_id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5yE2q3", "type": "review", "path": "docs/learned/cli/activation-scripts.md", "line": 106, "is_outdated": false, "classification": "actionable", "pre_existing": false, "action_summary": "Replace line number references (196-201, 202-225) with name-based source pointer to avoid drift", "complexity": "single_file", "original_comment": "**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk —
